title: geissorhiza divaricata
intro: Geissorhiza divaricata, also known as the Devil's Bean, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae, native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including being a natural remedy for fever reduction, inflammation, and as an antiseptic. Geissorhiza divaricata has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing pain, improving respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared for herbal use by boiling the roots or leaves in water, which can then be used as a decoction or infused in tea form.
